name: Hamilton Beach 1.7-Litre Variable Temperature Electric Kettle

specs.Temperature: 5 presets: 160°F, 175°F, 185°F, 190°F, 212°F (Green Tea, White Tea, Oolong, Coffee, Boiling) + Keep Warm

whatWorks: Five temperature presets cover the full range of hot drink preparation — 160°F for green tea (which burns and becomes bitter at boiling), 175°F for white tea and delicate senncha, 185°F for oolong, 190°F for French press coffee, and 212°F for full boiling. Having accurate preset temperatures replaces the guessing game of letting boiling water cool down.

At $39 CAD, the Hamilton Beach delivers the core function of a variable-temperature kettle — accurate presets, fast boiling, and Keep Warm — at a fraction of the $150+ price of Fellow Stagg and similar premium models. For tea and coffee drinkers who want correct temperatures without spending premium pricing, this is the value entry point.

Boil time from cold to 212°F with a full 1.7L is approximately 7 minutes at 1500W — fast enough that daily use doesn't require planning ahead. For half-capacity (4 cups), boil time drops to under 4 minutes, which is genuinely quick for morning routines.

The stainless steel interior prevents plastic taste transfer that affects cheaper plastic-lined kettles. Both the body and lid interior are stainless, which is not universal at this price — many budget kettles use plastic interior surfaces that impart taste to the water over time.
worthKnowing: Temperature accuracy varies ±5°F from preset values, which is adequate for tea and coffee but not for precision applications like specialty tea ceremonies or laboratory use. Premium kettles like the Fellow Stagg EKG maintain ±2°F. At $39, the variance is acceptable and real-world invisible in a cup.

The Keep Warm function holds temperature for 30 minutes. After 30 minutes the kettle shuts off, requiring a reheat cycle. If you steep multiple pots of tea over an extended afternoon and want continuous warm water, this requires attention.

The exterior plastic housing is not stainless steel — only the interior is stainless. The body is BPA-free plastic which is common at this price but means the exterior isn't as premium in feel as the interior quality suggests. The lid and handle are also plastic.

The water level indicator is on the side of the kettle and somewhat difficult to read when the kettle is on the base. A clearer or dual-side marking would improve ease of filling to an exact level — you end up estimating the fill more than reading a precise line.

commonComplaints.title: Temperature varies from preset — not precise…
commonComplaints.body: I measured it with a thermometer. The 175°F preset hit 172°F twice and 181°F once. Close enough for tea but not if you're obsessive about precision.

commonComplaints.title: Keep Warm only lasts 30 minutes…
commonComplaints.body: 30 minutes keep warm and it shuts off. If I'm steeping multiple batches of tea on a Saturday afternoon I'm reheating every 30 minutes. Wish it was longer.

commonComplaints.title: Exterior feels plasticky for the price…
commonComplaints.body: The inside is stainless but the outside is plastic and feels it. Doesn't bother me functionally but my partner hates how it looks on the counter.

commonComplaints.title: Water level window is hard to read…
commonComplaints.body: The level markings are on one side and faint. I consistently overfill it because I can't see the line clearly. Minor but annoying for daily use.

verdict: The Hamilton Beach Variable Temperature Kettle is the best-value electric kettle for Canadians who want accurate temperature presets for tea and coffee without paying premium pricing. At $39 it delivers the five temperatures that matter, a stainless interior, 1500W boil speed, and Keep Warm functionality — everything essential to a variable kettle, nothing unnecessary. Temperature precision isn't laboratory-grade, but it's more than adequate for every practical tea and coffee application at a price that's hard to argue with.

realBuyers.quote: My green tea never tastes bitter anymore. I used to boil and wait, always guessing. The 160°F preset hits exactly right every time.
Replaced a $150 kettle with this when it broke. Honest to god can't tell the difference in my coffee. The presets work.
Boils fast, keeps warm for my morning tea ritual, and tastes like water — not plastic. Everything I needed at $39.
The stainless interior is the reason I bought this over the cheaper models. No plastic taste. That's all I needed.

metaDescription: Hamilton Beach Electric Kettle review for Canada: 5 temperature presets, stainless interior, 1500W, Keep Warm. Honest on precision limits and exterior feel. ~$39 CAD.
introText: The Hamilton Beach 1.7L Variable Temperature Electric Kettle offers five tea and coffee temperature presets — from 160°F for green tea to boiling — with a stainless steel interior and Keep Warm function. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B007THHFT0) at around $39 CAD.
